meaning: to be big; to be tall; to be loud

expressions.english: big money; a large amount of money
expressions.notes: 돈 = money

expressions.english: big shoes
expressions.notes: 신발 = shoes

expressions.english: to be tall
expressions.notes: 키 = height

expressions.english: (someone) is big
expressions.notes: 덩치 = one's body size

expressions.english: big difference in daily temperature
expressions.notes: 일교차 = daily temperature difference

expressions.english: there's a high possibility; there's a good chance
expressions.notes: 가능성 = possibility

expressions.english: to speak loudly; to speak up
expressions.notes: 말하다 = to talk; to say; to speak; to tell

expressions.english: Is it really big?
expressions.notes:
많이 = to be a lot
많이 is an adverb form of 많다.

expressions.english: How big is it?
expressions.notes: 얼마나 = how + adjective/adverb

expressions.english: It's not that big.
expressions.notes:
별로 = not particularly, not very, not so much, not really
안 = not

expressions.english: This is too big.
expressions.notes:
이거 = this thing; this
이건 is the contracted form of 이거는.
너무 = too much, excessively; very

expressions.english: The bigger, the better.
expressions.notes:
-(으)면 -(으)ㄹ수록 = the more..., the more...
좋다 = to be good, likable; to be desirable; to be nice; to like

expressions.korean: 더 큰 가방이 필요할 것 같아요.
expressions.english: I think I need a bigger bag.
expressions.notes:
더 = more
가방 = bag
필요하다 = to need; to be necessary
-(으)ㄹ 것 같다 = I think + S + will/be going to; it seems like + S + will/be going to

expressions.korean: 저는 그렇게 큰 돈을 벌어 본 적이 없어요.
expressions.english: I've never made so much money.
expressions.notes:
저 = (polite) I; me
그렇게 = like that; such + adjective/adverb
돈을 벌다 = to make/earn money
-이/이/ 본 적이 있다 = to have not + p.p.

expressions.korean: 인터넷으로 봤을 때는 이렇게 클지 몰랐어요.
expressions.english: When I saw it on the Internet, I didn't know it was going to be this big.
expressions.notes:
인터넷 = the Internet
보다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up
-(으)ㄹ 때 = when/while + S + V
이렇게 = like this, in this manner; so +adjective
모르다 = to not know

expressions.korean: 요즘 일교차가 커서 감기에 걸리는 사람이 많아요.
expressions.english: Since there's such a big difference in the daily temperature these days, many people are catching colds.
expressions.notes:
요즘 = these days, lately
-이/이/어서 = because, since
감기 = cold, flu
감기에 걸리다 = to catch a cold
사람 = person, people

expressions.korean: 저도 직접 본 적이 없어서 얼마나 큰지 모르겠어요.
expressions.english: I didn't see it for myself, so I don't know how big it is.
expressions.notes:
-도 = too, also
직접 = directly; by oneself; with one's own hands
-(은)는 것이 있다 = to have never + p.p.

expressions.korean: 예전 사무실보다 새 사무실이 훨씬 크다고 들었어요.
expressions.english: I heard the new office is a lot bigger than the old one.
expressions.notes:
예전 = before; previous
사무실 = office
-보다 = than + N
새 = new
훨씬 = a lot/far/much (+ more + adjective/adverb)

expressions.korean: 이 파일은 용량이 커서 이메일로는 보 내기 힘들 것 같아요.
expressions.english:
This file is big in volume, so I think it's difficult to send via e-mail.
This file is huge, so I think it's difficult to send it via e-mail.
expressions.notes:
이 = this
파일 = file; folder
용량 = capacity; volume
이메일 = e-mail
-기 힘들다 = to be difficult/hard to + V

expressions.korean: 지금처럼 차가 계속 막히면 1시간내로 도착 못 할 가능성이 크죠.
expressions.english: If the traffic continues to be so backed up, there's a good chance that we won't be able to arrive within an hour.
expressions.notes:
지금 = now
-(으)ㄹ 것 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V
-시간 = one hour
-처럼 = like + N
차 = car
차가 막히다 = the traf-fic is bad; the road is jammed with traffic
-내 = within (+ time)
도착하다 = to arrive
못 = can't
